label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's break this down step by step to figure out whether following these instructions brings us back to the starting point. First, the instructions are: 1. Take 7 steps. 2. Turn around. 3. Turn around. 4. Turn around. 5. Take 1 step. 6. Take 6 steps. Let me track my position and direction as I follow these instructions. Let's assume I'm facing North at the beginning. - Step 1: Take 7 steps North. I'm now 7 units North of the starting point, still facing North. - Step 2: Turn around. I'm now facing South. - Step 3: Turn around. I'm now facing North again. - Step 4: Turn around. I'm now facing South again. - Step 5: Take 1 step. Since I'm facing South, I take 1 step South. I'm now 7 - 1 = 6 units North of the starting point. - Step 6: Take 6 steps. I'm still facing South, so I take 6 steps South. I'm now 6 - 6 = 0 units from the starting point, so I'm back at the starting point. So according to this reasoning, the answer would be "Yes" - following these instructions does return to the starting point. Let me double-check: 1. Take 7 steps in the initial direction (say North): +7 North 2. Turn around: facing South 3. Turn around:
